verdicts.issue: Author field is reversed: 'Research, ABI' parses as surname=Research, given=ABI. Should be a corporate-author form '{ABI Research}'.
Pages field reads '483' but actual paper spans 483-485 per AFIPS Conference Proceedings Vol. 30 (1967). Single-page value is incorrect.

Original ProPublica 'Machine Bias' article is from May 2016 (key year matches that). Entry cites the 2022 reprint in 'Ethics of Data and Analytics' (Auerbach Publications) with date 2022-03-16. The chapter form is legitimate but the date/year disagree with the key year by 6 years; key suggests 2016 original but body describes 2022 reprint. Would need a publisher decision on which to cite.

suggested_fix.note: If the citation refers to the original ProPublica article, switch to a @misc/@online entry citing https://www.propublica.org/article/machine-bias-risk-assessments-in-criminal-sentencing with date 2016-05-23.

verdicts.issue: Author 'Ltd., Arm' is reversed: parses as surname='Ltd.', given='Arm'. Should be corporate-author form '{Arm Ltd.}' or '{Arm}'. Also key 'ARM2020' uses uppercase prefix that doesn't match conventions.

Journal field 'SSRN Electronic Journal' refers to the SSRN preprint mirror, not the canonical published venue. The paper was published in California Law Review, vol. 104, pp. 671-732 (2016). Pages and volume are correct but journal name is wrong.

URL https://dspace.mit.edu/handle/1721.1/12962 resolves to a different thesis ('Wages and capital' by George A. Akerlof, 1966 economics PhD), not Bobrow's STUDENT thesis. Correct DSpace handle is 1721.1/6903.

Author list incomplete: entry has 'Cai, Han and Gan, Chuang and Han, Song' (3 authors) but the actual ICLR 2020 paper has 5 authors: Han Cai, Chuang Gan, Tianzhe Wang, Zhekai Zhang, Song Han. Two authors (Tianzhe Wang, Zhekai Zhang) are missing.

suggested_fix.author: Cai, Han and Gan, Chuang and Wang, Tianzhe and Zhang, Zhekai and Han, Song

verdicts.issue: Author list is wrong. Entry lists 'Cai, Tianle and Li, Yuhong and Geng, Zhengyang and Peng, Sheng and Liu, Jingjing and Smith, David and Shavit, Nir' but the actual arXiv 2401.10774 / ICML 2024 paper authors are: Tianle Cai, Yuhong Li, Zhengyang Geng, Hongwu Peng, Jason D. Lee, Deming Chen, Tri Dao. The last four authors in the entry (Sheng Peng, Jingjing Liu, David Smith, Nir Shavit) are fabricated/incorrect.

suggested_fix.author: Cai, Tianle and Li, Yuhong and Geng, Zhengyang and Peng, Hongwu and Lee, Jason D. and Chen, Deming and Dao, Tri

verdicts.issue: Author 'Systems, Cerebras' is reversed (parses as surname='Systems'); should be corporate '{Cerebras Systems}'. URL cerebras.net/wp-content/... redirects to cerebras.ai/wp-content/... but liveness of the redirected PDF was not fully verified.

Author 'Systems, Cerebras' is reversed; should be '{Cerebras Systems}'. URL https://cerebras.ai/product-chip/ redirects to a generic WSE-3 marketing page (current product is WSE-3, not WSE-2), so URL no longer resolves to the cited WSE-2 content.

suggested_fix.author: {Cerebras Systems}
suggested_fix.note: Locate original WSE-2 whitepaper or Hot Chips 33 paper if that is the intended reference.

verdicts.issue: Author field is wrong. Entry lists 'Chapelle, O. and Scholkopf, B. and Zien, Eds., A.' as authors, but Chapelle/Scholkopf/Zien are the EDITORS of the 2006 book being reviewed. The 2009 review article (IEEE Trans. Neural Networks 20(3):542) was authored by Philippe Thomas. The key 'chapelle2009semisupervised' suggests a citation of the textbook itself; if so, the entry should be a @book to the 2006 MIT Press volume rather than the 2009 review, with editors Chapelle/Scholkopf/Zien.

suggested_fix.author: Thomas, Philippe
suggested_fix.title: Review of "Semi-Supervised Learning" by O. Chapelle, B. Scholkopf, and A. Zien (MIT Press, 2006)
suggested_fix.note: Or, if intent is the textbook itself, switch to @book{chapelle2006semisupervised, editor = {Chapelle, Olivier and Sch{\"o}lkopf, Bernhard and Zien, Alexander}, title = {Semi-Supervised Learning}, publisher = {MIT Press}, year = {2006}}

verdicts.issue: Year/volume/issue/pages/DOI all incorrect. Entry shows year=2016, volume=51, number=1, pages=186-198, doi=10.1109/JSSC.2015.2488709. The actual Eyeriss JSSC paper by Chen, Krishna, Emer, Sze appeared in IEEE J. Solid-State Circuits vol. 52, no. 1, pp. 127-138, Jan. 2017, DOI 10.1109/JSSC.2016.2616357. The cited DOI 10.1109/JSSC.2015.2488709 returns 404.

suggested_fix.note: Key 'chen2016eyeriss' may be retained (the ISSCC short paper appeared 2016); if renaming to chen2017eyeriss, update every [@chen2016eyeriss] reference in book/quarto/contents/**/*.qmd.

verdicts.issue: Author list is wrong. Entry lists 'Chen, Yanxi and Liu, Xuchen and Ding, Yaliang and Wang, Chenkai and Li, Huiqiang and You, Meng and Luan, Zhiqiang and Qian, Depei and Li, Yaliang' (9 authors). Actual ICML 2024 paper authors are: Yanxi Chen, Xuchen Pan, Yaliang Li, Bolin Ding, Jingren Zhou (5 authors). Several listed names (Liu/Ding/Wang/Li/You/Luan/Qian) are fabricated; correct surnames include Pan, Li, Ding, Zhou.

suggested_fix.author: Chen, Yanxi and Pan, Xuchen and Li, Yaliang and Ding, Bolin and Zhou, Jingren

_notes: Stamping conventions: 94 entries in this chunk are stamped 'pass-17-bib-hygiene', 3 'pass-16-bib-sweep', 2 'citation-cleanup', 1 'gemini-3.1-pro-preview' (skipped). Per audit instructions, only the gemini stamp triggers skip.

Several entries have both 'journal' and 'booktitle' fields populated (e.g. aws_sagemaker, brewer2000towards, ardila2020common, akidau2015dataflow, banbury2021mlperftiny, ba2014deep, bahdanau2014neural, brown2020language, breck2019data, buolamwini2018gender, amershi2019software, bergstra2010theano, chen2015mxnet, chen2024eellm). Not flagged as broken individually since fields are correct, but bibtex-tidy may resolve duplicates inconsistently.

Multiple entries omit booktitle/journal venue expansion per bib-check.md §2 (e.g. chen2024eellm uses 'Proceedings of the 41st International Conference on Machine Learning' without parenthetical (ICML); cai2024medusa uses 'International Conference on Machine Learning' without (ICML); chen2020simclr same; Cai2020 uses 'International Conference on Learning Representations' without (ICLR)). Not individually marked broken; would be a separate venue-expansion sweep.
Cerebras2021 and Cerebras2021wse2 share the 'Systems, Cerebras' reversed-author bug; both flagged uncertain pending the user's decision on canonical Cerebras whitepaper URLs.
